salary_range.note: Artisan wages to small bangle workshop owner

growth_outlook: Stable - festive and bridal demand
entry_paths: Apprentice in a Firozabad or Jaipur bangle unit
Family craft transfer / SHG training
Supply to wholesalers or sell at melas and online
qualifications: No formal degree - hands-on apprenticeship
Craft/SHG training programme (optional)

title: Numerologist
description: Advise clients using numerology - name/number analysis, lucky numbers, name-correction and business naming - increasingly popular via apps, YouTube and matrimony/branding consults.

salary_range.note: Local practice to celebrity numerologist with online following

growth_outlook: Growing - digital astrology/occult market expanding
entry_paths: Learn from a guru or certified numerology course
Start free readings to build reputation, then charge consults
Build a YouTube/Instagram presence and join astro platforms
qualifications: Numerology certification/diploma (private institutes) - optional
No formal degree required for private practice

title: Palmist (Hast Rekha Shastra)
description: Read palms (Hast Rekha) - lines, mounts and hand shape - for guidance on career, relationships and health, often alongside astrology. Demand at fairs, online and private consults.

salary_range.note: Local readings to established palmistry consultant

growth_outlook: Stable to growing - steady traditional demand
entry_paths: Study under an experienced palmist or via books/courses
Practise readings at fairs/exhibitions to gain experience
Offer online consultations and build a following
qualifications: No formal degree - apprenticeship/self-study
Palmistry course certificate (optional)

title: Tarot Card Reader
description: Give tarot card readings for guidance on love, career and decisions - a fast-growing urban and online practice among younger Indians, popular on Instagram and consultation apps.

salary_range.note: Part-time readings to full-time tarot consultant/creator

growth_outlook: Growing fast - popular with urban youth online
entry_paths: Take a tarot certification course or learn from a mentor
Practise with friends, then offer paid online readings
Grow via Instagram Reels and consultation platforms
qualifications: Tarot certification (private/online) - optional
No formal degree required

title: Reiki / Pranic Healer
description: Practise energy healing (Reiki, Pranic Healing) for stress, wellness and relaxation - often combined with meditation and offered at wellness centres or independently.

salary_range.note: Part-time healer to wellness-centre practitioner/trainer

growth_outlook: Growing - wellness and holistic health demand
entry_paths: Get attuned and certified through Reiki Levels 1-3 / Master under a teacher
Practise on clients and at wellness centres
Offer distance healing and workshops online
qualifications: Reiki Level certifications (I, II, Master)
Pranic Healing course (optional)
No formal degree required

title: Mehndi / Henna Artist
description: Apply mehndi (henna) designs for weddings, festivals and events - bridal, Arabic, and contemporary styles. High festive/wedding-season demand and strong social-media discoverability.

salary_range.note: Per-function fees to bridal mehndi brand with a team

growth_outlook: Good - steady wedding and festive demand
entry_paths: Practise extensively and build a design portfolio
Start with small events, then bridal bookings
Promote on Instagram and tie up with wedding planners
qualifications: No formal degree - skill and portfolio matter most
Mehndi/beauty course certificate (optional)

title: Rangoli & Floor Art Artist
description: Create rangoli and floor art for festivals, weddings, events and brand activations - including large 3D and themed rangolis. Seasonal but high-visibility creative work.

salary_range.note: Per-event fees to commissioned festive art business

growth_outlook: Stable - festive and event demand
entry_paths: Build a portfolio of festival and event rangolis
Offer services for Diwali/Onam/weddings and corporate events
Share time-lapse videos on social media to get bookings
qualifications: No formal qualification - creativity and portfolio
Fine arts background (optional)

title: Classical Dancer (Bharatanatyam / Kathak)
description: Perform and teach Indian classical dance such as Bharatanatyam, Kathak, Odissi or Kuchipudi. Income blends stage performances, teaching a dance school, choreography and cultural programmes.

salary_range.note: Performance fees + teaching; varies widely by reputation

growth_outlook: Stable - cultural and teaching demand
entry_paths: Train for years under a guru (guru-shishya parampara)
Complete graded exams / Arangetram (debut performance)
Build income via performances, teaching and cultural bodies
qualifications: Visharad / degree in dance (e.g. from a music-dance university) - optional but respected
Years of guru-led training and a public debut (Arangetram)

title: Folk Artist / Lok Kalakar
description: Perform regional folk art forms - dance, music, theatre such as Bhavai, Lavani, Bihu, Garba, or storytelling - at festivals, tourism events and cultural programmes, often with government and tourism support.

salary_range.note: Programme fees; supported by govt cultural schemes

growth_outlook: Stable - government and tourism patronage
entry_paths: Learn the form within community/family tradition
Register with cultural/tourism departments and Sangeet Natak Akademi
Perform at festivals, tourism circuits and cultural exchanges
qualifications: No formal degree - community/traditional training
Empanelment with cultural bodies (ICCR, state akademis)

title: Dhol / Percussion Artist
description: Play dhol, nagada, or other percussion at weddings, processions, festivals and events. High demand in the wedding and DJ/event circuit, especially in North and West India.

salary_range.note: Per-event fees to busy wedding/event percussionist

growth_outlook: Good - strong wedding/event demand
entry_paths: Learn from senior players / community groups
Join a dhol/baraat troupe for events
Build bookings via wedding planners and social media
qualifications: No formal degree - apprenticeship and practice
Music school percussion training (optional)

title: Puppeteer (Kathputli & Modern)
description: Perform and craft puppets - traditional Kathputli string puppets or modern puppetry - for shows, education, tourism and media. A niche heritage art with workshop and edutainment demand.

salary_range.note: Show fees; supplemented by workshops and tourism

growth_outlook: Niche - heritage and edutainment demand
entry_paths: Learn within a puppeteer community (e.g. Rajasthan) or arts org
Perform at schools, fairs, tourism and cultural events
Run puppet-making workshops as an added income stream
qualifications: No formal degree - traditional/community training
Theatre or fine-arts background (optional)

title: Equestrian / Horse Riding Instructor
description: Train riders and care for horses at riding schools, clubs, resorts and for equestrian sport. Combines coaching, horse management and sometimes competition or wedding/film horse work.

salary_range.note: Riding school instructor to own stable/academy

growth_outlook: Niche but growing - clubs, resorts and sport
entry_paths: Train at a riding school / cavalry or police equestrian background
Get coaching experience at clubs or resorts
Build towards running your own stable or academy
qualifications: Equestrian Federation of India coaching credentials (for sport)
No formal degree required - skill and experience matter

title: Stunt Performer / Action Choreographer
description: Perform film/TV stunts and choreograph action sequences - fights, falls, fire, vehicle and wire work. High-skill, high-risk work in film, OTT, ads and events.

salary_range.note: Per-shoot fees; top action directors earn much more

growth_outlook: Good - growing film/OTT/ad production
entry_paths: Train in martial arts/gymnastics and join a stunt union/team
Start as a junior stunt artist and build credits
Progress to action choreographer/director
qualifications: Stunt training and union membership (e.g. Movie Stunt Artists Association)
No formal degree - physical skill and safety record matter

title: Mountaineering / Trekking Guide
description: Lead treks, mountaineering expeditions and adventure tours in the Himalayas and beyond, ensuring safety and a great experience. Demand driven by India's booming adventure-tourism market.

salary_range.note: Seasonal guiding to running an adventure travel firm

growth_outlook: Growing - adventure tourism boom
entry_paths: Complete courses at NIM/HMI/ABVIMAS mountaineering institutes
Guide with established adventure operators
Build towards leading expeditions or your own travel firm
qualifications: Basic/Advanced Mountaineering Course (NIM, HMI, ABVIMAS)
Wilderness first-aid certification

title: Scuba Diving Instructor
description: Teach and guide scuba diving at coastal/island destinations (Andamans, Goa, Lakshadweep) - certifying divers and leading dives. Tied to coastal tourism growth.

salary_range.note: Resort instructor to dive-centre owner

growth_outlook: Growing - coastal adventure tourism
entry_paths: Progress through PADI/SSI: Open Water -> Divemaster -> Instructor
Work at a dive centre in a coastal destination
Build towards owning a dive centre
qualifications: PADI/SSI Instructor certification
Strong swimming ability; medical fitness

title: Wildlife Photographer
description: Photograph wildlife and nature for magazines, documentaries, stock, NGOs and prints, often working from India's national parks and sanctuaries. Income mixes assignments, workshops, stock and tours.

salary_range.note: Assignments + prints/stock; varies with reputation

growth_outlook: Niche - conservation media and tourism
entry_paths: Build a strong portfolio at national parks and sanctuaries
Sell to magazines, stock platforms and NGOs
Run photography tours and workshops for steady income
qualifications: No formal degree - portfolio is the credential
Photography course or naturalist training (optional)

title: Wildlife Rescuer / Snake Catcher
description: Rescue and safely relocate snakes and urban wildlife, educate communities, and assist forest departments. A specialised, public-service-oriented field tied to conservation and human-wildlife conflict.

salary_range.note: Often honorarium/NGO-paid; some run rescue services

growth_outlook: Niche - rising urban wildlife conflict
entry_paths: Train with experienced rescuers and herpetology groups
Volunteer with NGOs and forest department
Build a recognised rescue service in your area
